headline: 'We must hold our heads high!': The fears and faint hopes of Israel's peace activists
description: Every day, a few thousand NGO members act in support of peaceful coexistence with Palestinians, a halt to settlements and an end to the military occupation of the West Bank. Yet, their political and social influence has been steadily declining over the past 20 years.

headline: Germany's Zverev wins French Open
Thousands march in silence in tribute to killed 11-year-old girl in France
Armenians vote in pivotal legislative elections for their country's future, regional peace
description: Sunday's vote serves as a referendum on peace with Azerbaijan and on closer ties with the European Union and the US at the expense of Russia. This geopolitical shift is spearheaded by Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan, in office since 2018 and leading in the polls.

headline: France revives Gratitude Train for the US's 250th anniversary
description: A restored railcar from northern France will retrace the journey of the Gratitude Train, which carried gifts from Le Havre to New York in 1949.

headline: Women's desire, a men's issue?
description: No, women should not feel pressured to boost their libidos when they wane, writes sex columnist Maïa Mazaurette, who urges both men and women to reconsider female desire from a societal perspective.

headline: China's all-round dominance, from batteries to medicine, from high-speed trains to AI
description: 'How China is Devouring Europe' (2/4). Beijing has caught up with and then surpassed the West across an impressive number of technologies and economic sectors, seeking to control entire value chains.

headline: 'No one was prepared for what was about to happen': Gaza flotilla activist speaks about violence she suffered in Israel
Iran's World Cup squad lands in Mexico as US visa row rages
French telecom giants reach deal to acquire struggling rival SFR
Israeli army reports missile attack from Iran in response to its strike on Beirut's southern suburbs
Pope Leo XIV quotes Gandalf, highlighting Tolkien's close ties with Catholicism
Kanye West draws crowd in Netherlands despite anti-Semitic outings
Mirra Andreeva defies all odds to win first Grand Slam at French Open
Sagrada Familia organist to lead landmark Mass as Pope Leo XIV visits Spain
With 'De Gaulle: Tilting Iron,' Simon Abkarian boldly steps into the General's shoes
Putin's 'Davos' in Saint Petersburg ends as it began, under a barrage of Ukrainian drones
Mélenchon holds first campaign rally in Saint-Denis as part of identity-based 'new France' strategy
description: The election of Bally Bagayoko and Aly Diouara as mayors in the Paris-region department of Seine-Saint-Denis in March were symbolically significant for Jean-Luc Mélenchon. Since 2018, the radical-left leader has made the area a testing ground for his 'new France' concept, as he is counting on the vote of its working-class citizens from immigrant communities for next year's presidential election.

headline: Bernadette Chirac, the wife of ex-president Jacques Chirac, has died
description: An unfailing supporter of her husband's career, Bernadette Chodron de Courcel became a key figure a French politics. She was elected local councilor, a rare feat for a 'wife of,' and was very involved in charity work. She died at the age of 93.

headline: Over three decades, China's promise became a trap for European industry
description: 'How China is Devouring Europe' (1/4). In just a few decades, China has gone from being the world's factory to a major technological rival for Europeans, who had hoped to profit on China's vast domestic market but underestimated its capacity for innovation.
byline: Bastien Bonnefous,  Isabelle Chaperon and  Sophie Fay

headline: Outsourcing migrant deportations is incompatible with Europe's values
